A Trie is the perfect data structure for substring or prefix summing.
Instead of storing just an `isWord` flag at each Trie Node, you can store a `sum` integer.

When inserting a key, first calculate the difference between its new value and its old value (if it already existed). Then iterate through the characters of the key and add this difference to the `sum` attribute of every node along the path.

When querying `sum(prefix)`, simply traverse down the Trie following the prefix. If you reach the end of the prefix, return the `sum` stored at that node. If the prefix doesn't exist in the Trie, return 0.
